items.title: Google DeepMind Supports U.S. Department of Energy on Genesis — Google DeepMind
items.author: deepmind.google
items.snippet: Google DeepMind continues to publish significant research in early 2026 across mathematics, AI safety, and scientific discovery. A standout development is Gemini Deep Think, which has substantially outperformed prior IMO-Gold-level versions on Olympiad-level problems, and was used to autonomously generate a research paper (Feng26) calculating eigenweights in arithmetic geometry without human intervention. AlphaEvolve, a Gemini-powered coding agent, is being expanded through a partnership with th

items.title: Google DeepMind on X: Deep Think mode upgrade
items.author: x.com
items.snippet: Google AI and Google DeepMind have been active on social media in early April 2026, with several significant announcements. On April 2, 2026, Google upgraded its AI Pro subscription plan, expanding storage from 2TB to 5TB and introducing new Gemini-powered features including autonomous Chrome browsing, cross-platform chat import (from ChatGPT/Claude), and deeper Workspace integrations across Docs, Sheets, and Drive. Gemini 3.1 Flash Live was highlighted as Google's most advanced voice-first AI m

items.title: Gemini API: New Flex and Priority Inference Options | AIToolly
items.author: aitoolly.com
items.snippet: As of April 2026, Google has released several significant updates to the Gemini API. Most notably, new Gemma 4 open models (gemma-4-26b-a4b-it and gemma-4-31b-it) are now available via AI Studio and the Gemini API. Gemini 3.1 Flash Live Preview has launched as the latest audio-to-audio model for real-time dialogue applications. Veo 3.1 Lite Preview is now available as a cost-efficient video generation model, and Lyria 3 music generation models (lyria-3-clip-preview and lyria-3-pro-preview) have

items.title: Get Started with Genkit | Firebase - Google
items.author: firebase.google.com
items.snippet: Firebase Genkit is Google's open-source AI framework available for JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, and Python, designed to help developers build production-grade generative AI features. The official documentation at firebase.google.com/docs/genkit covers the full lifecycle: from initial setup (installing packages, configuring API keys, creating your first flow) to testing via the built-in Developer UI. Genkit provides a unified API surface that works across multiple model providers including Google G

items.title: Add Gemini 3.1 Pro to Default Google Provider Config · Issue #14310 · anomalyco/opencode
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: The google-gemini GitHub organization has seen significant activity in early 2026. The most notable development is the release of Gemini 3.1 Pro on February 19, 2026, featuring a 1 million token context window, 77.1% ARC-AGI-2 reasoning scores, and multimodal input support (text, image, PDF, video). Shortly after, on March 26, 2026, Google released Gemini 3.1 Flash Live — a real-time multimodal voice model optimized for low-latency audio, video, and tool use in AI agents. The gemini-cli reposito

items.title: Introducing Gemini 3 Flash: Benchmarks, global availability
items.author: blog.google
items.snippet: Google AI Studio's Antigravity platform has seen significant updates in early 2026. The platform launched as a full-stack agentic development environment, enabling developers to go from prompt to production-ready apps with features like multiplayer support, Firebase-backed databases and authentication, automatic dependency management (e.g., installing Framer Motion or ShadCN), and secure API key storage. Google positioned Antigravity as a major step in 'vibe coding' — turning natural language pr

items.author: firebase.google.com
items.snippet: Firebase Genkit is Google's open-source AI framework available for JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, and Python, designed to help developers build production-grade generative AI features. The official documentation at firebase.google.com/docs/genkit covers the full lifecycle: from initial setup (installing packages, configuring API keys, creating your first flow) to testing via the built-in Developer UI. Genkit provides a unified API surface that works across multiple model providers including Google G

items.title: Google models | Generative AI on Vertex AI | Google Cloud Documentation
items.author: docs.cloud.google.com
items.snippet: Imagen 4 is Google's highest-quality image generation model, available via two primary APIs: the Gemini API (via Google AI for Developers / AI Studio) and the Vertex AI API. On Vertex AI, it is currently in public preview and offers three variants: Imagen 4 for Generation (imagen-4.0-generate-001), Imagen 4 Fast Generation (lower latency), and Imagen 4 Ultra Generation (best prompt adherence and quality). All generated images are watermarked using SynthID, Google's invisible AI-content identific

items.title: Releases · firebase/genkit
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: Genkit is an open-source AI framework built and maintained by Google/Firebase for building AI-powered applications in JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, and Python. It provides a unified, code-centric API for integrating models from multiple providers (Google Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, etc.) with built-in support for structured output, agentic tool calling, RAG (retrieval-augmented generation), multi-modal input/output, and observability. Flows are the core abstraction — special functions with t

items.title: Function calling with a response mime type: application/json is unsupported - Gemini API Forum
items.author: discuss.ai.google.dev
items.snippet: The Gemini API provides two related but distinct mechanisms for obtaining structured data: Structured Outputs and Function Calling. Structured Outputs allow developers to constrain model responses to a specific JSON Schema by setting `response_mime_type` to `application/json` and supplying a `response_schema` in the generation config. The Google GenAI SDKs support schema definition via Pydantic (Python) and Zod (JavaScript), making it straightforward to get type-safe, parseable responses from th
